criteria.label: State the sigmoid formula, BCE formula, and compute both on a toy example.
criteria.example: Given w=2, b=−1, x=1: z=1, ŷ=σ(1)≈0.73. For y=1: BCE=−log(0.73)≈0.31. For y=0 on same ŷ: BCE=−log(0.27)≈1.31.

criteria.label: Explain why logistic regression is classification despite the name.
criteria.example: Write log(ŷ/(1−ŷ)) = wᵀx + b and note output is P(y=1|x) ∈ (0,1) with BCE loss — contrast with linear regression ŷ = wᵀx + b and MSE on continuous y.

criteria.label: Implement or call a library fit that minimizes BCE and report probabilities plus thresholded labels.
criteria.example: Fit sklearn LogisticRegression on a 4-row XOR-infeasible linearly separable 2D set; print predict_proba[:,1] and predict; report log_loss on training data.

criteria.label: Identify the decision boundary and choose an evaluation metric for an imbalanced binary task.
criteria.example: For fraud (2% positive): state boundary wᵀx+b=0; argue for AUC and recall at tuned threshold over raw accuracy; cite class imbalance.
